div.head: 134. Editorial Note
div.p: On November 5, 1952, James S. Lay, Jr., Executive Secretary of the National Security Council, transmitted a “Key Data Book” prepared by the NSC Reporting Unit to President Truman. This briefing book contained sections on the national psychological program and the foreign intelligence program. For text, see Foreign Relations, 1952–1954, volume II, Part 1, pages 165–181.
